executive_summary.headline: Legacy Campaign Recovery in Progress
executive_summary.situation: After 4 months of RocketClicks' high-intent campaigns underperforming, legacy campaigns were reactivated on Feb 22. Best week ever followed (Feb 22-28), but March shows volatility as Google's algorithm re-learns.
executive_summary.key_insight: The March 'collapse' is actually a NORMAL re-learning period. Your campaigns are rebuilding from 4 months of neglect.

phase_1.title: RocketClicks High-Intent Era
phase_1.description: RocketClicks ran high-intent campaigns that the algorithm prioritized over legacy campaigns
phase_1.result: Legacy campaigns deprioritized, Quality Scores decayed, historical conversion data staled

phase_2.title: Legacy Campaign Reactivation
phase_2.description: High-intent campaigns paused, legacy Nationwide Non-Brand campaigns reactivated
phase_2.result: BEST WEEK EVER - Algorithm still had historical patterns, Quality Scores hadn't fully degraded

phase_3.title: Re-Learning Period
phase_3.description: Google's Smart Bidding starts making new predictions with limited recent data
phase_3.result: CPL volatility as algorithm explores optimal bid levels

phase_4.title: Recovery & Optimization
phase_4.description: Negative keywords added, informational queries blocked

why_march_collapsed.explanation:
During the 4 months RocketClicks ran their high-intent campaigns, your legacy campaigns were deprioritized.

Google's algorithm stopped serving impressions to your proven keywords, causing their Quality Scores to decay.
Quality Score is based on:
- Expected CTR (drops when ads don't show)
- Ad relevance (loses freshness signals)
- Landing page experience (no new data)

Currently 146 keywords have QS below 5. This means you're paying 50-400% MORE per click than competitors with higher QS.

Top affected keywords:
• 'how to start a nonprofit' (QS: 2) - $6998.85 spend
• 'how to start a nonprofit' (QS: 3) - $5140.76 spend
• 'how to start a nonprofit' (QS: 3) - $3142.07 spend
• 'how to open a 501c3' (QS: 1) - $2483.00 spend
• 'start a nonprofit' (QS: 3) - $2228.85 spend
why_march_collapsed.impact: Estimated 30-50% higher CPCs due to QS penalty
why_march_collapsed.fix: Quality Scores take 2-4 weeks to rebuild with consistent impressions. Keep campaigns running.

why_march_collapsed.explanation:
During the 4 months of neglect, Google's broad match algorithm started matching your ads to increasingly irrelevant queries.

Without active negative keyword management, informational searches like "how to start a nonprofit" began triggering your ads. These searchers are in RESEARCH mode, not BUYING mode.

462 informational queries spent $20317.30 in the last 30 days with minimal conversions.

Examples:
• 'how to start a nonprofit' - $1075.84
• 'how to start a non profit' - $738.93
• 'how to start a nonprofit organization' - $619.20
• 'how to start a non profit organization' - $595.14
• 'how to start a nonprofit' - $517.01
• 'how to start a nonprofit organization' - $401.47
• 'how do i start a non profit organization' - $370.85
• 'how to start a non profit' - $342.77
why_march_collapsed.fix: Added 175 negative keywords to block these queries. Effect will be immediate.

why_march_collapsed.explanation:
When you paused the RocketClicks campaigns and reactivated the legacy campaigns on Feb 22, Google's Smart Bidding algorithm had to re-learn.

The algorithm needs 2-4 weeks of consistent data to optimize effectively. During Feb 22-28, it was still using historical conversion patterns from when the campaigns were active.

By March, the algorithm started making new predictions based on recent (limited) data, causing volatility.

The spike days (March 4: $117 CPL, Feb 27: $141 CPL) are the algorithm "exploring" - testing higher bids to gather conversion data.
why_march_collapsed.impact: CPL volatility of 50-100% during learning period
why_march_collapsed.fix: Allow 2-3 more weeks for algorithm to stabilize. Avoid making major changes.

why_march_collapsed.explanation:
Your campaigns are only capturing 21-43% of available impressions:

• Nationwide - Non Brand 1: 23% impression share
• Nationwide - Non Brand 2: 35% impression share
• Weekend campaigns: 21-43% impression share

This means 57-79% of potential leads are going to competitors.

Low impression share is caused by:
1. Budget limitations (hitting daily caps)
2. Ad rank issues (QS × bid)
3. Competition increasing bids

During the 4-month RocketClicks period, competitors may have increased their presence.
why_march_collapsed.fix: Consider 20-30% budget increase on best performers (Weekend campaigns at $62-74 CPL)

why_march_collapsed.explanation:
Late February/early March often sees shifts in nonprofit formation searches:

• Tax season drives 501(c)(3) interest
• New Year resolution effect fading
• Q1 business planning cycles

This doesn't explain the full drop but contributes to conversion rate variance.

actions.reason: Block informational queries that waste budget on non-converting traffic

actions.detail: Campaign 'Non Brand - High Intent (FL Ad Group LP Test)' is a trial campaign and cannot be paused via API. Recommend manual pause in Google Ads UI.
actions.reason: This campaign from RocketClicks had only 2 conversions on $847 spend

immediate.action: Manually pause High-Intent campaign in Google Ads
immediate.reason: It's a trial campaign that API cannot modify

immediate.action: DO NOT make major changes for 2-3 weeks
immediate.reason: Let Smart Bidding re-learn with consistent data

short_term.action: Increase budget on Weekend campaigns by 30%
short_term.reason: Best CPL ($62-74) with low impression share (21-43%)

short_term.action: Review landing pages for low QS keywords

expected_outcomes.week_1_2: Informational queries blocked, immediate waste reduction of ~$500/day
expected_outcomes.week_3_4: Smart Bidding stabilizes, CPL variance reduces by 50%
expected_outcomes.week_5_8: Quality Scores begin recovering, CPCs decrease 10-20%
expected_outcomes.month_3: Full optimization potential - targeting $65 CPL with current budget
